# Soma Language — Complete Reference for AI Agents

> Soma is a fractal, declarative, agent-native language for verified distributed systems.
> The compiler is the agent's collaborator: it fixes errors, catches anti-patterns, and auto-serializes data.

## Agent Workflow

```
1. Generate code      → write .cell file
2. Auto-fix errors    → soma fix app.cell (adds missing handlers, fixes properties)
3. Lint               → soma lint app.cell (catches anti-patterns)
4. Check contracts    → soma check app.cell --json
5. Verify state       → soma verify app.cell --json
6. Serve              → soma serve app.cell -p 8080
```

## Quick Syntax Rules

- No semicolons. Newlines separate statements.
- No `function`/`def`. Use `on handler_name(params) { }`.
- No `null`. Use `()` for null/unit.
- No `[1,2,3]`. Use `list(1, 2, 3)`.
- No `{key: val}`. Use `map("key", val, "key2", val2)`.
- No `import`. Use `use lib::module`.
- No `console.log`. Use `print(value)`.
- No `===`. Use `==`.
- Strings: `"hello {name}"` (interpolation with `{}`).
- `if`/`match` are expressions: `let x = if cond { a } else { b }`.
- Last expression in handler is the return value (implicit return).

## Cell Structure

```soma
cell AppName {
    face {
        signal create(name: String) -> Map   // contract: MUST have handler
        promise "all data is tracked"
    }
    memory {
        data: Map<String, String> [persistent, consistent]  // -> SQLite
        cache: Map<String, String> [ephemeral]              // -> in-memory
        invariant _slot_len <= 10000                         // checked on every .set()
    }
    state workflow {
        initial: draft
        draft -> active { guard { _from == "draft" } }
        active -> completed
        * -> cancelled
    }
    every 30s { cleanup() }
    after 5s { initialize() }

    on create(data: Map) {
        let id = to_string(next_id())
        data.set(id, data)                // auto-serializes maps
        ensure data.len > 0               // postcondition
        map("id", id, "status", "ok")     // implicit return
    }

    on request(method: String, path: String, body: String) {
        let req = map("method", method, "path", path)
        match req {
            {method: "GET", path: "/"}                    -> html(home())
            {method: "GET", path: "/api/" + resource}     -> list(resource)
            {method: "POST", path: "/api/" + resource}    -> create(resource, body)
            {method: "DELETE", path: "/api/" + resource}   -> delete(resource)
            _ -> response(404, map("error", "not found"))
        }
    }
}
```

## Match Patterns (all composable)

```soma
match value {
    "literal"                    -> expr          // literal
    "a" || "b"                   -> expr          // or-pattern
    name                         -> use(name)     // variable binding
    "/api/" + rest               -> api(rest)     // string prefix
    {method: "GET", path}        -> get(path)     // map destructuring
    {method: "POST", path: "/api/" + r} -> post(r) // nested destructure
    0..17                        -> "minor"       // range pattern
    n if n > 100                 -> "big"         // guard clause
    ()                           -> "null"        // unit/null
    _                            -> "default"     // wildcard
}
```

## Types

| Type    | Example                              |
|---------|--------------------------------------|
| Int     | `42`, `-1`                           |
| Float   | `3.14`, `1.5e3`                      |
| String  | `"hello {name}"`                     |
| Bool    | `true`, `false`                      |
| List    | `list(1, 2, 3)`                      |
| Map     | `map("key", val, "key2", val2)`      |
| Unit    | `()` (null equivalent)               |
| Duration| `5s`, `1min`, `500ms`, `1h`          |

## Storage (auto-serializes)

```soma
// Store maps directly — no to_json needed
data.set("key", map("name", "Alice", "score", 95))

// Read back as map — no from_json needed
let user = data.get("key")
print(user.name)   // "Alice"

// Other methods
data.delete("key")
data.keys             // list of keys
data.values           // list of values
data.len()            // count
data.has("key")       // bool
```

## Pipes

```soma
data |> filter(x => x.score > 50)
data |> map(x => x.name)
data |> sort_by("score", "desc")
data |> top(10)
data |> reduce(0, p => p.acc + p.val)
data |> filter_by("price", ">", 100)
data |> group_by("dept")
data |> distinct("category")
data |> with("new_field", value)
data |> join(other, "key")
```

## Error Handling

```soma
let result = try { risky_operation() }
if result.error != () { return response(500, map("error", result.error)) }

// Short form: ? propagates errors
let value = try { operation() }?

// Postconditions
ensure balance >= 0   // fails if false
```

## CLI Commands

| Command                         | Description                                        |
|---------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| `soma run file.cell [args]`     | Execute signal handler                             |
| `soma serve file.cell [-p port]`| HTTP server                                        |
| `soma check file.cell [--json]` | Verify contracts + properties                      |
| `soma fix file.cell`            | Auto-repair errors (missing handlers, bad props)   |
| `soma lint file.cell [--json]`  | Catch anti-patterns                                |
| `soma verify file.cell [--json]`| Prove state machine properties                     |
| `soma describe file.cell`       | Rich JSON: handlers, memory, state, face           |
| `soma test file.cell`           | Run test cells                                     |
| `soma init [name]`              | Create project                                     |

## soma check --json output

```json
{
  "passed": false,
  "errors": [
    {
      "kind": "missing_handler",
      "message": "face contract: signal 'delete' declared in cell 'App' has no handler",
      "fix": "Add a handler to cell 'App':\n\n    on delete() {\n        return map(\"status\", \"ok\")\n    }"
    }
  ]
}
```

## soma describe output

```json
{
  "cells": [{
    "name": "App",
    "handlers": [
      {"name": "create", "params": [{"name": "data", "type": "Map"}], "is_public": true, "line": 10}
    ],
    "memory": [
      {"name": "items", "type": "Map<String, String>", "properties": ["persistent"]}
    ],
    "state_machines": [
      {"name": "order", "initial": "pending", "transitions": [{"from": "pending", "to": "confirmed", "has_guard": true}]}
    ],
    "face": {"signals": [{"name": "create", "params": ["data: Map"], "returns": "Map"}]},
    "scheduled": [{"kind": "every", "interval_ms": 30000}]
  }]
}
```
